_comment: Synthetic governance emails for the B.B.A. AACSB reaffirmation. 'restricted' sensitivity items (accreditation-confidential liaison correspondence) are visible only to personas listed in acl. thread groups related mail.

emails.subject: CONFIDENTIAL: AACSB site-visit timeline and evidence requests — B.B.A.
emails.body: Laura, Provost — confidential pre-decisional note from the AACSB liaison. The peer-review team has set the site visit for the week of 12-OCT. They specifically asked us to prepare: (1) closed-loop assurance-of-learning evidence for the revised B.B.A. assessment plan, and (2) documentation that the program-review committee approved the related course-sequence change. They flagged the assurance-of-learning gap as the single biggest risk to reaffirmation. Keep this to leadership until we brief the committees.

emails.subject: RE: CONFIDENTIAL: AACSB site-visit timeline and evidence requests
emails.body: Paul — understood and owned. We will close the assurance-of-learning loop on the revised B.B.A. assessment plan and produce the program-review sign-off evidence for the course-sequence change ahead of the 12-OCT visit. I'll create tracked tasks for each outstanding item and brief both committees. Treating this as leadership-confidential until the brief.

emails.subject: Assurance-of-learning actions still open from the last two committee meetings
emails.body: Colleagues — flagging that two assurance-of-learning actions are still open. From the curriculum committee: close the loop on the revised B.B.A. written-communication rubric (assigned to me). From the program-review committee: confirm the course-sequence change is reflected in the catalog. Both are stalled waiting on the institutional-research data pull, which Ravi expects this week. We should not let these slip into the site-visit window.

emails.subject: RE: Assurance-of-learning actions still open
emails.body: Ada — the assessment data pull lands Friday. Once you have it you can close the written-communication rubric loop. I'll send the program-review committee the catalog comparison the same day.

emails.subject: Covering B.B.A. site-visit prep while you're travelling
emails.body: Laura — confirming the live B.B.A. site-visit items: the curriculum committee's revised assessment-plan decision, the open assurance-of-learning actions, and the program-review committee's course-sequence sign-off. The accreditation tracker should be the source of truth for outstanding tasks.

emails.subject: B.B.A. governance weekly — action movement w/c 08-JUN
emails.body: Weekly note: the curriculum committee approved the revised B.B.A. assessment plan; two assurance-of-learning actions remain open pending the assessment data pull. The program-review committee has the course-sequence change on its agenda for sign-off. No other governance movement this week.
